resolution_strategies.change_description: Copy the existing blacklist activity into the due-diligence failure branch so that when the condition 'not data.evaluation' becomes true inside the loop, 'add respondent bank to blacklist' is guaranteed to occur before the process can continue or re-evaluate the condition.

change_risk.reason: The change is localized to the branch already associated with a failed due-diligence evaluation and does not affect the annual report activities or the direct risk-based termination path. The main risk is possible duplicate blacklist execution if the later existing blacklist branch also executes, but this is preferable to missing the required blacklist action and can typically be handled as an idempotent business action.

resolution_strategies.change_description: Prevent the due diligence activity from writing the loop-control variable data.evaluation_extra, so the condition is not made true by an activity that is not prior to the evaluated loop condition.

change_risk.reason: The change is localized to the data written by one activity and does not alter the main control-flow structure. It preserves the existing annual report activities and the direct risk-to-terminate behavior. The main impact is that additional due diligence iterations can no longer be triggered by the due diligence activity itself.
